title: Tempting Rich Japanese Scallops Mushimono
description: A spicy, creamy, and indulgent Japanese dish featuring red wine jelly and steamed scallops in their shells, perfect for a special occasion. This kosher-friendly recipe combines the tender flavor of scallops with the richness of red wine and cream.
instructions: Rinse the scallops in cold water and pat them dry with paper towels.
In a small saucepan, combine the red wine, soy sauce, sake, mirin, grated ginger, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper, and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer the sauce for 5 minutes, or until it has thickened slightly.
Stir in the heavy cream and continue to simmer for another 2 minutes, or until the sauce has coated the back of a spoon.
Remove the sauce from the heat and let it cool to room temperature.
Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Place a scallop in each shell and spoon some of the cooled sauce over the top of each scallop.
Cover the scallops with plastic wrap or aluminum foil and steam them over boiling water for 4-6 minutes, or until they are cooked through.
Remove the scallops from the steamer and garnish each one with chopped green onions.
Serve the scallops immediately, with additional sauce spooned over the top if desired.
